commit.message:
Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Differentiate Chat/SQL/Agent modes: skip charts in Chat and SQL modes

Chat mode now returns SQL + results + one-line explanation only, skipping
the chart generation AI call for faster responses. SQL mode returns results
only with no AI calls at all. Agent mode retains full analysis with charts.
Adds visual mode indicators to QueryChat: amber-tinted input border and
badge for Agent mode, different placeholder text and empty state messaging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fetchState() was blindly following transitions on normal screens

fetchState() checked `if (data.transition)` without verifying that the
response had no screen content. When a normal screen (e.g. city hub)
contained WebApp buttons (Mochila, Explorar), the serializer extracted
the first one as `transition`. fetchState() would then redirect to that
WebApp instead of rendering the city screen — causing an infinite loop
back to inventory when pressing the back button.

Fix: Add `&& !data.text` guard to match returnFromWebApp() behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add authoritative data guard to check-in prompts (moved task contamination)

LLM was referencing tasks from earlier conversation messages even after
they were moved to future dates. All data sources (Task.due_date,
CalendarEvent.start_dt) were correct, but the LLM prioritized conversation
history over current system prompt data.

Added explicit instruction: only reference items in the current data
sections, ignore contradictory info from earlier messages.

commit.message:
Fix isValidCodeAddress overlap bug and add trusted seed filtering

Two critical fixes for disassembly coverage:

1. PlatformDescription.isValidCodeAddress(): MAME-generated platform XMLs
   have catch-all IO regions (0x0-0xFFFFFFFF) that overlap specific ROM/RAM
   regions. The old first-match logic rejected valid ROM addresses. Now
   checks ALL regions and returns true if ANY is rom/ram.

2. HeuristicCodeFinderAnalyzer: Vector table seeds (Pass 2) and reference
   targets (Pass 3) now use trusted=true, which skips entropy, null density,
   and minBlockSize filters. These seeds are high-confidence (from vector
   table or CALL/BRANCH targets) and shouldn't be rejected by data filters
   designed for speculative orphan recovery.

   Also raised entropy filter minimum block size from 16 to 64 bytes for
   untrusted blocks, since entropy is unreliable on small samples.

Impact: DN5500 boot ROM goes from 930 to 4490 instructions (4.8x), with
Pass 3 reference tracing finding 2846 instructions across 11 iterations
(previously 0, because failed Pass 2 seeds never discovered CALL targets).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Bi-Amp crossover block to signal chain

Insert Linkwitz-Riley 4th-order (LR4) crossover after compressor.
When ON: LP output feeds clean DI, HP output continues through
amp chain — enabling true bi-amping via DI Blend. Crossover
frequency adjustable 60–500Hz. OFF reverts to full-band split.

commit.message:
fix(ui): use updated member_info for notifications so sender name is correct

The notification system was capturing member_info BEFORE applying the
state delta, so when a new member sent a message (with their member_info
included in the same delta), the notification would show "Someone"
instead of their actual nickname. Fix both code paths (apply_delta and
update_room_state) to use member_info from the updated state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Discord bot 3-period alerts + obligation sync from JSON

discord-bot/models.py:
  - Add periodo_dias (Integer, nullable) to Obligacion — stores full
    period length so tasks.py can compute 3 equidistant alert dates

discord-bot/sync_from_json.py (new):
  - Reads contract_data*.json from repo root (same source as dashboard)
  - Computes next due date + period_days; skips conditional obligations
  - Upserts into obligaciones table using deterministic UUID5 keys
  - Maps dashboard areas → bot DB constraint values
  - Ensures deudas row exists per bank; safe to re-run

discord-bot/tasks.py:
  - Replace fixed thresholds (90/30/15/3d) with 3-period equidistant:
      Alert 1 → due_date − ⌊period × 2/3⌋  (early warning)
      Alert 2 → due_date − ⌊period × 1/3⌋  (midpoint reminder)
      Alert 3 → due_date                    (deadline day)
  - AlertaEnviada dedup prevents duplicate alerts per cycle
  - Legacy threshold path kept for obligations without periodo_dias

discord-bot/alerts.py:
  - Add PERIOD_COLORS (Unergy brand: #2C2039 / #915BD8 / #F6FF72)
  - Add PERIOD_LABELS for alert_num 1/2/3
  - Add build_period_alert_embed() and send_period_alert()
  - Legacy send_alert() unchanged for backwards compatibility

discord_alerts.py (standalone webhook fallback, no bot required):
  - Reads same JSON files, same 3-period logic
  - Sends via Discord incoming webhook (no DB needed)

Note: GitHub Actions workflow requires a token with `workflow` scope.
Create .github/workflows/discord_alerts.yml manually via GitHub web UI.

Patch 8 audit findings: security, data integrity, UX

Security:
- Service areas DELETE now requires org_id match (prevents cross-org deletion)
- Survey allocations validated against launching program's org

Data integrity:
- Clamp canAfford to 0 minimum (negative value inflated shortfall)
- Guard division by zero in reach % calculations (6 locations)
- CSV export now respects year filter (client + server)
- availableYears includes survey-only years in dropdown

commit.message:
Cache classify candidates to avoid recomputing on every page request

classify_candidates() (the expensive cdist call) now runs once and is
stored in app.state.classify_cache keyed by (threshold, min_size, since,
until).  Page navigation serves directly from cache.  The cache is
invalidated when labels are submitted, or when any query parameter
changes (e.g. threshold slider).

commit.message:
Address code review: bugs, security, UX improvements (issue #2, #3)

Bug fixes:
- Add 2-minute max recording duration with auto-stop
- Fix race condition on rapid mic button taps (300ms guard)
- Add clipboard fallback feedback toast in overlay service
- Limit custom dictionary to 200 words (Whisper prompt limit)
- Guard stopRecording() against double-calls

Security:
- Clean up WAV files after transcription (privacy)
- Expanded ProGuard/R8 rules for OkHttp, Tink, coroutines

API improvements:
- Specific error messages for 401/403 (invalid API key)
- Retry with exponential backoff on 429/5xx errors (max 3)
- AuthenticationException and RetryableException classes

UX enhancements:
- Haptic feedback on recording start (30ms) and stop (50ms)
- Show audio duration during transcription ("Transcribing... (3s audio)")
- Track last inserted text for future undo feature
- TalkBack/accessibility: contentDescription on all keyboard buttons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add well-known docs for cross-ecosystem name collisions

Add 16 new entries to _WELL_KNOWN_DOCS for libraries whose common name
collides with wrong packages on other registries (rails, laravel, symfony,
echo, flutter, swift, nginx) or differs from their actual package name
(nextjs, vuejs, angular, trpc, tanstack-query, turborepo, react-query).

Bump DISCOVERY_VERSION 25 -> 26 to invalidate cached results.

commit.message:
fix: rate limiter uses X-Forwarded-For for real client IP

Behind Railway's reverse proxy, request.client.host returns the proxy
IP, not the real user. This made rate limiting ineffective in production.

Next.js static export doesn't copy _-prefixed files from public/ to out/.

rewrite rule before the :id catch-all since Cloudflare _redirects rules
fire before static file lookup (contrary to docs).
